name: Santa Catarina (Brazil)

colors.symbolism: elements of the central coat of arms
elements of the central coat of arms

description: Three horizontal bands of white, red (twice as wide) and white, with a green lozenge at the centre bearing the state coat of arms.
history: Adopted by Decree No. 1.181 on 29 October 1953; the design is by Henrique Boiteux and dates from earlier proposals first used in the 1890s under the First Republic.

features.symbolism: Three horizontal bands, white, red and white, with a green lozenge centred over the red band bearing the state coat of arms

features.role: state coat of arms within a green lozenge
features.symbolism: a green diamond bearing the coat of arms of Santa Catarina, with sheaves of wheat, a key and a Phrygian cap symbolising agriculture, security and the Republic
